methodsAttempted.query: site:ufl.edu University of Florida generative AI policy students faculty artificial intelligence
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl official-domain search found the UF AI Guidelines page, student guidance, instructor guidance, a DOCX best-practices file, and a CITT teaching page.
The ai.ufl.edu Guidelines page is an official UF AI site page from the Office of the Provost and includes teaching, student, research, HR, safety, and approved-tools guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ai.ufl.edu/guidelines UF AI Guidelines Office of the Provost
methodsAttempted.notes: Direct HTTP fetch confirmed the official Guidelines page returned HTTP 200 and contained the needed substantive source text.

artifacts.summary: Official-source-only discovery found a comprehensive University of Florida AI Guidelines page on ai.ufl.edu. Separate student and instructor guidance pages were checked but rejected for this bundle as duplicate/narrower surfaces because the Guidelines page contains the same relevant student and instructor guidance plus broader research, safety, HR, and tools sections.

targets.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Use of AI at UF

stopConditions: do_not_use duplicate/narrow pages when comprehensive official guidance is available
do_not_stage raw html docx pdf or screenshots

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Use of AI at UF

artifacts.claimText: UF AI guidance says users should use UF-approved or UF-provided tools for sensitive or restricted data and says restricted or sensitive data is not permitted with cloud models tagged External in NaviGator Chat.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Use UF-approved or UF-provided tools for sensitive/restricted data; restricted or sensitive data is not permitted with External-tagged cloud models in NaviGator Chat.

artifacts.title: University of Florida AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: The University of Florida has an official AI Guidelines page from the Office of the Provost with university-wide guidance across teaching, learning, research, HR, safety, security, and AI tools. This staged bundle approves three narrow claims on sensitive/restricted data handling, course-level AI-use categories, and IRB approval for AI-related human-subject research.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Separate UF student and instructor guidance pages were accessible but treated as duplicate/narrower surfaces because the comprehensive Guidelines page includes the relevant material.
The course-use category claim is limited to general approaches because the source explicitly says those considerations are not formal statements or policies.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
